articles.headline: Sephora Copies A Walmart Move Shoppers Love
articles.author: Maurie Backman
articles.summary: If you’ve ever walked into a Sephora location before, you may have experienced the same thing I did — sensory overload. Of course, for some beauty enthusiasts, there’s nothing more exciting than stepping into a massive store that’s loaded with just about every skin care and makeup product imaginable. For someone like myself whose beauty routine consists mostly of ChapStick, the main reason you’ll find me inside a Sephora is because one of my daughters has asked, or because I’ve stopped in to buy a gift.

articles.headline: Walmart Among Chains Forced By The Government To Give Away Food For Free Under New ‘Recovery’ Law
articles.author: Grace Harrington
articles.summary: WALMART is among the chains that will need to donate food for free under a proposed law put forward by a state government. Chains would also need to document how much food they give away. A proposed Connecticut law seeks to address food insecurity by allowing food retailers like grocery stores to donate surplus food. The bill would require the state’s Departments of Consumer Protection and Public Health to develop and implement guidelines for retailers to donate surplus food.

articles.headline: Walmart Makes Another Move To Win Higher-Income Shoppers
articles.author: Madison Troyer
articles.summary: For the last few months, Walmart has been working hard to change shoppers’ perceptions. The retailer spent decades establishing itself as a value destination, offering rock-bottom prices on everyday goods, but has recently put forth a concerted effort to appeal to higher-income earners. It’s a strategy that appears to be working. Executives have repeatedly highlighted growing market-share gains among households earning more than $100,000, and the retailer has drastically expanded its assortment of premium brands across fashion, beauty and home.

articles.headline: Coresight: Walmart, Amazon Cross Major Market-Share Milestone
articles.author: Marianne Wilson
articles.summary: Accelerating market-share consolidation among top-tier retailers has become a “defining story” of the U.S. retail competitive landscape. That's according to new data from Coresight Research, which found that for the first time, two retailers crossed the threshold of capturing more than 10% of total U.S. retail sales. The frontrunner was Walmart, accounting for 11.08% of U.S. retail sales in 2025.

articles.headline: Heating Up Walmart’s Chaotic ‘Route’ Change In Stores Leaves Furious Shoppers With ‘Melting’ Groceries
articles.author: Grace Harrington
articles.summary: An update in Walmart’s Spark delivery app has left angry customers with melted groceries and workers complaining of slow delivery times. The change affects the routes delivery workers take in-store. Workers who use Spark, Walmart’s shopping and home delivery service, say the app’s new policy making the shopping process more inefficient. Delivery drivers told Business Insider that the app has changed its feature that maps out a route to pick up items in store.

articles.headline: Walmart’s 7,200 Price Cuts Land Heaviest In One Category
articles.author: Damilola Esebame
articles.summary: Walmart has lowered prices on a range of grocery staples, including ground beef, corn on the cob, cherries, and 24-packs of soda. The nation’s largest retailer recently unveiled a summer rollback campaign covering thousands of products across groceries, household essentials, and seasonal goods nationwide. Walmart had about 7,200 rollbacks in place during its fiscal first quarter ending April 30, up more than 20% year over year, and roughly half of them targeted food items, Walmart Chief Financial Officer John David Rainey told Yahoo Finance.

articles.headline: Walmart Reveals Soccer Fans’ World Cup Shopping Habits
articles.author: Mitchell Parton
articles.summary: Data is starting to trickle in about how consumers’ shopping habits shifted during the World Cup. A new study from Walmart shows how its soccer-watching consumers planned to grocery shop during the World Cup, from how much they would spend to what kind of snacks they would buy — and the company has seen some of these results validated over the course of the tournament. About half of consumers who identified as soccer fans expected to increase grocery spending by at least 25% during major match weeks, Walmart found through surveys in June, according to its analytics arm Walmart Data Ventures. These surveys included 1,021 both casual and dedicated soccer fans who were members of the Walmart Customer Spark Community.

articles.headline: Walmart Agrees To Police Mercury Content In Skin Lighteners Under NGO Settlement
articles.author: Eileen Francis
articles.summary: The US Food and Drug Administration has stepped up its efforts to combat skin-lightening products containing banned mercury above trace levels, but a legal settlement between Walmart Inc. and NGO the Mercury Policy Project highlights the challenges faced by retailers and resellers confronting the products still on the market. The settlement came shortly after FDA issued an advisory cautioning consumers of sky-high mercury levels found...

articles.headline: Walmart EV Charger Deployments Exploded In Q2. Only Tesla Installed More Plugs
articles.author: Suvrat Kothari
articles.summary: America's electric vehicle fast-charging market keeps throwing surprises every quarter. Despite the reset in EV sales momentum, the fast-charging network continues to grow at a rapid clip. New players like Ionna have ramped up quickly, deploying hundreds of new ports in just about a year since entering the market. But retail giant Walmart is coming in hot with its own charging ambitions, posting some serious numbers for Q2. According to EV charging data platform Paren, Walmart outranked every charging network except Tesla in second-quarter deployments. Walmart added 368 new ports in Q2, behind Tesla's 1,185 but ahead of ChargePoint (333), Red E (315) and Electrify America (202). That means Walmart alone accounted for 8.4% of new public fast-charging stations in the U.S. during the quarter, even though its all-time market share is still below 1%.

articles.headline: Walmart Cuts Prices Again. Small Businesses Should Do The Opposite
articles.author: Micah Logan
articles.summary: Walmart has strategically cut prices on thousands of items in its stores to help customers cope with inflation over the last few years. News like this could be tempting for Small Business Owners to copy as a pricing strategy in their own businesses; however, price matching is not always a winning strategy. It’s often a race, pun intended, to the bottom. In a 2025 survey of Small Business Owners, 66% say that big retailers are their fiercest competitors. The real business operator question shouldn’t be, "How do I compete on price?" but "Why am I tempted to pull that lever first?" There are five plays that Business Owners can use instead of competing on price, which could prove disastrous to their bottom line.

articles.headline: Walmart Bets On AI And Digital Twins To Shape Its Supply Chain Strategy
articles.author: Paige Gross
articles.summary: Before Walmart kicked off an effort to get AI into the hands of 2 million employees, the retail giant’s supply chain team already relied on the technology to help get products where they needed to go. Predictive models have always had a home within the supply chain industry, Indira Uppuluri, Walmart’s SVP of supply chain technology, told CIO Dive. But the amount of data and AI tools her teams have access to now gives them stronger signals to navigate a sometimes challenging landscape, Uppuluri said — “That’s where the industry is going.”

articles.headline: US Retail Sales Extend Growth Streak As June Spending Rises
articles.author: Mohamed Dabo
articles.summary: US retail sales continued to grow in June, marking a ninth straight month of gains as consumers maintained spending despite ongoing economic uncertainty. US retail sales increased for the ninth consecutive month in June, with shoppers continuing to spend despite economic uncertainty, according to the latest CNBC/NRF Retail Monitor. The report, released by the National Retail Federation (NRF) and based on anonymised credit and debit card transactions compiled by Affinity Solutions, showed total retail sales excluding automobile dealers and petrol stations rose 0.33% month on month on a seasonally adjusted basis.

articles.headline: Massive 7-Eleven Shakeup Will Close Hundreds Of Stores Across America This Year
articles.author: Grant Butler
articles.summary: For millions of Americans, it’s the kind of convenience store that’s become part of the daily routine – a quick stop for coffee on the way to work, a late-night snack run or a fill-up at the pump. But behind the familiar storefronts, the retail giant is preparing for a major shake-up that will affect hundreds of locations across the country. 7-Eleven is planning to close or convert 645 locations, according to the company’s first-quarter earnings presentation.

articles.headline: US Retail Sales Rise 9.41% YoY In June, Clothing Leads Growth
articles.summary: The United States total retail sales, excluding automobile dealers and gasoline stations, increased 0.33 per cent seasonally adjusted from May and 9.41 per cent unadjusted from June 2025, according to the CNBC/National Retail Federation (NRF) Retail Monitor, powered by Affinity Solutions. In May, the monitor had reported gains of 0.42 per cent month on month (MoM) and 7.19 per cent year on year (YoY).

articles.headline: Walmart Pursues Plan For Mammoth Distribution Center In Orange County
articles.author: Peter Katz
articles.summary: Walmart has revived and updated a plan for a mammoth warehouse and distribution center at 146 and 150 Route 17K in the Orange County Town of Wallkill. Plans for WalMart Stores Inc. to build a slightly smaller distribution center had been approved by Wallkill in 2003. In 2017, another application was made to Wallkill for the warehouse and distribution center but nothing was constructed. The company has now submitted revised plans to the Wallkill Planning Board that call for a warehouse and distribution center of 1.5 million square feet that would employ 810 people during each shift with two shifts per day.

articles.headline: Walmart Grocery Delivery Workers Have Issue With New App Tool
articles.author: Bill Wilson
articles.summary: Walmart has updated its Spark delivery worker app in a way that has led to confusion and delays, Business Insider reports. A mapping feature called Smart Path has changed the way workers navigate stores to fulfill orders. The app used to provide workers with a route around the store for each order to help with efficiency. The update, however, tracks workers’ locations in the store and rearranges the list of items based on their proximity. In many instances, workers pick up perishables and frozen food first rather than at the end.

articles.headline: Walmart Rival Slashes Prices Up To 10% On 300 Items Like Snacks And Produce Through Labor Day
articles.author: Jacob Willeford
articles.summary: A MAJOR competitor to Walmart has slashed prices on more than 300 products for customers in honor of Labor Day. It isn’t that uncommon for retailers to either launch sales for the holiday or close down locations to give employees time off. Giant Eagle, which has more than 197 supermarkets in Ohio, Pennsylvania, Maryland, Indiana, and West Virginia, is averaging around 10% discounts on merchandise as of July 9, per a press release. The chain has launched the seasonal promotion as part of its campaign that started in 2025 to remind shoppers that it can offer them serious value.

articles.headline: Walmart Heads Back-To-School With Emphasis On Value, Convenience
articles.summary: Walmart said it is posting its lowest back-to-school prices since 2019 on the most popular school supplies, affordable solutions for every stage of the scholastic journey and healthy meals to fill lunchboxes as the company also touts delivery and pickup conveniences.

articles.headline: Walmart Teams With General Mills, ADM On Regenerative Agriculture Initiative
articles.author: Julia Tabisz
articles.summary: Walmart is teaming up with General Mills and ADM, a wheat supplier that manages nearly 5 million regenerative acres globally, to accelerate regenerative agriculture practices across 40,000 wheat acres in Illinois, Indiana and Missouri, the companies said Wednesday. The initiative spans across the supply chain, with a focus on growing regions where General Mills sources wheat from ADM for products sold through Walmart and Sam's Club, the companies said. Along with ADM, Walmart and General Mills will give farmers technical assistance and financial incentives to adopt practices that improve soil health, water quality and carbon sequestration, such as no-till farming and cover crops.

articles.headline: Worker Says Walmart Fired Her After She Reported Racial Slurs
articles.author: Tez Romero
articles.summary: A Walmart worker says she was fired after she reported a supervisor's racial remarks about her biracial baby and a co-worker's sexual harassment. That is the core of a complaint an employee filed on July 14, 2026, in the US District Court for the Northern District of Florida, accusing Wal-Mart Stores East, LP of race discrimination, sexual harassment, and retaliation under Title VII and Section 1981. The employee worked as a Fresh Team Associate in the produce and deli department of a Pensacola Supercenter, according to the filing. She says her problems began not long after she transferred there in December 2024.

articles.headline: Walmart Quietly Rolls Out Camouflaged ‘Pedestal’ Anti-Theft Measure Secretly Watching Shoppers
articles.author: Joseph Brogan
articles.summary: A HIDDEN anti-theft measure is keeping a close eye on Walmart shoppers, and they may not even realize. The big-box retailer’s new surveillance pedestals are cleverly disguised to look like anything but an anti-theft tool. Walmart has used electronic article surveillance pedestals for years, but is now changing their appearance on salesfloors. The technology is now being branded with Walmart colors and logos, hiding the otherwise visible sensors and surveillance technology underneath.

articles.headline: Walmart Accelerates AI And Automation Investment To Transform Retail Operations
articles.summary: Walmart is stepping up its investment in artificial intelligence, automation and advanced digital technologies as the world’s largest retailer continues to modernise its operations and strengthen its position in an increasingly competitive global grocery market. The company is expanding the use of AI across multiple areas of its business, including inventory management, demand forecasting, supply chain optimisation and customer service. By integrating advanced technology into daily operations, Walmart aims to improve efficiency, reduce costs and ensure products are available when customers need them.

articles.headline: The State Of Agentic Storefronts: How AI Agents Guide Shoppers On Frictionless, Full-Funnel Journeys
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence is the new entry point in e-commerce, as more shoppers rely on AI-powered summaries and assistants to discover products. Beyond discovery and product recommendations, AI agents are also helping shoppers evaluate options, make decisions and even complete purchases. According to Salesforce data, AI and agents drove 20% of all retail sales and accounted for $262 billion in holiday spend during the 2025 holiday shopping season. To keep up, brands and retailers are implementing agentic tools to better personalize shopper experiences and drive growth. Others are rethinking the traditional e-commerce model from the ground up — shifting from static websites to agentic storefronts with conversational experiences that guide customers every step of the way.

articles.headline: US Retailers Turn To New Tools As Fraud Becomes More Complex
articles.author: Mahamed Dabo
articles.summary: US retailers are adopting a new industry framework to better understand and respond to changing fraud risks as criminals use more advanced methods across stores and digital channels. The National Retail Federation (NRF) has released an updated retail fraud taxonomy, a classification system designed to help retailers, technology companies, financial organisations and public agencies use a common language when identifying fraud threats. The update reflects a changing retail environment where fraud is no longer limited to traditional theft. Businesses now face a wider range of challenges, including online scams, payment abuse, account takeovers and organised criminal activity.

articles.headline: Shoppers Choose Mass Retailers Over Traditional Grocers For BTS Supplies: Report
articles.author: Samantha Schober
articles.summary: Grocery chains are losing back-to-school shoppers to mass merchandisers this year, even as the overall BTS season is set to grow to $36.1 billion, according to two new reports from Coresight Research. Walmart will capture 59% of BTS shoppers in 2026, up from 56% last year, while Target holds steady at 39% and Amazon trails at 38%, per Coresight’s June 29 survey of 510 U.S. consumers with children aged 5-17. Mass merchandisers overall drew 67.8% of shoppers, a lead of 22.3 percentage points over online-only retailers in second place.
